text_draft:
Susie came to the window. She pressed her small hand against the glass next to Pink Bear's velvet nose.

"Mom said the eggs are already hidden," Susie whispered. "Outside. Under the flowers."

Pink Bear's button heart sank. The eggs were out there. Buried. Lost beneath the snow.
Susie's shoulders slumped. "We can't find them now."

Pink Bear looked at her girl. Looked at the snow. Looked back at Susie.

There had to be a way.
